title: The One Where No One Proposes

jokes.text: Hey look! It's my good friend Ross. Hey Ross... Hey and look he brought flowers. Thanks Ross, but I'm really more of a candy guy.
jokes.setup: Joey awkwardly overenthusiastic greeting suggests he's covering something
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets flowers as gift for him instead of for Rachel/baby
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's self-centeredness and social obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey, hey, hey! How's it going, man? You look great! New haircut?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, Joey, I brought these flowers for Rachel and the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Flowers? For me? Oh man, you shouldn't have! What's the occasion?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They're not for you, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh come on, don't be shy. They're beautiful. I love you, man.

jokes.text: You're weird today.
jokes.setup: Joey has been acting extremely strange
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement given Joey's bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understatement that lands with economy

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I mean, Joey's been acting strange, but this is... this is a massive understatement.

jokes.text: Joey, can you give us just a minute? / No. / What? / Oh, I'm sorry. I meant no.
jokes.setup: Standard polite request for privacy
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt refusal, then fake-apologizing for the bluntness while maintaining the refusal
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - perfect structure with the fake apology that changes nothing

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Could you give me some privacy?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, that was rude. No.

jokes.text: Oh, Aunt Liddy's coming? That means we get five dollars each!
jokes.setup: News that elderly relative is visiting
jokes.punchline: Excitement is purely mercenary - she gives small cash gifts
jokes.explanation: Relatable family dynamic joke with good specificity

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Aunt Ruth is coming to visit!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's great! You must be so excited to see her.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I am! She always gives me fifty bucks!

jokes.text: He pulled a quarter out of my ear!
jokes.setup: Grandfather has been practicing magic tricks
jokes.punchline: Already performing basic magic on adults, treating them like children
jokes.explanation: Simple visual comedy that establishes character trait

dialogue.speaker: Grandfather
dialogue.line: Watch closely as I make this coin disappear!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dad, we've all seen that trick before.

dialogue.speaker: Grandfather
dialogue.line: Ah, but have you seen it THIS way? Now the coin is behind your ear!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, a coin. Behind my ear. I'm amazed. Truly.

jokes.text: Oh and I need to talk to you. / About what? / To see if know what these guys are talking about.
jokes.setup: Everyone needs to have important talks
jokes.punchline: Joey wants to talk just to find out what everyone else is talking about
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - completely self-interested and transparent about it

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I want to have an important talk too!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, but everyone else is having one, so I figured I'd jump in and see what we're all talking about.

jokes.text: What about Ben? / Well of course Ben, I meant my first granddaughter.
jokes.setup: Jack declares Emma his 'first grandchild'
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction when reminded of Ben's existence
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward family moment about favoritism

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Emma is my first grandchild.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dad, what about Ben?

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Oh right, Ben. Well, Emma is my first granddaughter.

jokes.text: Oh sure okay, you can touch yourself in front of us but you can't talk to Rachel. / What?! When have I ever touched myself in front of you guys?
jokes.setup: Monica making analogy about Ross's comfort level
jokes.punchline: Ross's horrified confusion at the accusation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Ross's genuine confusion

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're like a little old man. You like your apartment at a certain temperature, your books organized a certain way, your life planned out.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I do not!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're like Mr. Geller.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I am nothing like Dad!

jokes.text: Oh please! Just before when you were asleep in the lounge!
jokes.setup: Ross denying touching himself in front of them
jokes.punchline: Specific recent incident cited
jokes.explanation: Escalates the embarrassment with specificity

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I did not touch myself in front of you people!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What about last week when you were watching that nature documentary?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That was different! I was adjusting my pants!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: For forty-five minutes?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It was a really long documentary!

jokes.text: That Armenian family was watching you instead of the TV.
jokes.setup: Ross was touching himself in his sleep
jokes.punchline: Random family found it more entertaining than television
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with vivid, absurd imagery that's highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A random family found it more entertaining than television.

jokes.text: That was you?! I thought it was Jack!
jokes.setup: Joey confessing to doing something terrible
jokes.punchline: Chandler assumes it's about the masturbation incident
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection callback that connects storylines

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler, I have to tell you something. I did something really terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is this about the time you... you know?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because we agreed never to speak of that again.

jokes.text: As we all are at some point during the day.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining he was on one knee with a ring
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan suggestion this is normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - absurdist logic delivered with complete deadpan confidence. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was on one knee with a ring.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's how most proposals go.

jokes.text: This is gonna kill him! You know how much he loves to propose!
jokes.setup: Concern about Ross's reaction to Joey's accidental proposal
jokes.punchline: Treating proposal as Ross's hobby given his history
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's romantic history with fresh perspective

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you know, with Ross's history of marriage proposals, maybe we should just treat this like one of his hobbies. Like how he collects fossils or obsesses over paleontology.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not a hobby, Chandler, that's a lifestyle.

jokes.text: Now let's not get carried away.
jokes.setup: Chandler warns Ross might kick Joey's ass
jokes.punchline: Joey's mild concern about Ross being physically threatening
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his confidence in handling Ross physically

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ross? Kick my ass? Please. I could take Ross with one hand tied behind my back.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and the other hand would be tied behind his back.

jokes.text: Honey, we've been over this. I need to be facing the other way.
jokes.setup: Monica says 'I want a baby'
jokes.punchline: Chandler thinks she means right now and references sexual position
jokes.explanation: Strong misdirection with perfect structure and timing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Right now? Should I get the table out of the way?

jokes.text: Yeah okay, but no foreplay. / Deal!
jokes.setup: Monica wants to have sex again to increase pregnancy chances
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exhausted negotiation and Monica's eager agreement
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing their different energy levels

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could we maybe just try once? I'm not saying every day, but maybe like... once a week?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Once a week? Chandler, we need to do it every day. Every other day at least!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Every day? Monica, I'm not a machine. I'm more of a... occasional appliance.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you're going to have to become a daily appliance, because I want to get pregnant!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know you want to get pregnant, but I also kind of want to live.

jokes.text: Dad seriously!
jokes.setup: Jack apparently doing something inappropriate off-camera
jokes.punchline: Monica's exasperated reaction to unseen behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that sets up family dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: I like to think of myself as the puppet master of the group.
jokes.setup: Monica taking credit for Joey's proposal
jokes.punchline: Grandiose self-image as manipulator
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character moment showing her control freak nature

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I orchestrated the whole thing. I planted the idea in Chandler's head, I told Rachel where to be, I even convinced Phoebe to sneeze at the exact right moment so Joey would turn around. It was like conducting a symphony of romance, and I was Maestro Geller.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You didn't do any of that.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But I could have. That's the point. I'm that good.

jokes.text: The puppet master gets tired people.
jokes.setup: Rachel wants Monica to convince her too
jokes.punchline: Monica maintaining the puppet master persona while complaining
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that extends the joke

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you have to convince me too.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not doing this anymore. I'm tired of being the puppet master.

jokes.text: Although he does play with himself in his sleep. / I can't say that I'm surprised.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Joey's sleep habits
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unsurprised reaction
jokes.explanation: Solid callback with Phoebe's characteristic acceptance of weird behavior

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey fell asleep during the movie.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that's not surprising. Joey sleeps like 16 hours a day.

jokes.text: It can't be me, I'm standing right here.
jokes.setup: Jack thinks people are having sex in a room
jokes.punchline: Logical elimination of himself as suspect
jokes.explanation: Good logical humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Wait, who's in that room? It's not me... I'm out here talking to you.

jokes.text: Y'know what? I don't like you without mom.
jokes.setup: Jack being inappropriate and voyeuristic
jokes.punchline: Monica realizing her mother keeps him in check
jokes.explanation: Relatable family observation with good character insight

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you mean my mom is the only thing keeping my dad from being a total creep?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. Without her, he'd be pressed against the glass at Bloomingdale's.

jokes.text: Dear Lord.
jokes.setup: Nurse instructing Rachel to open up and put breast in mouth
jokes.punchline: Joey's uncomfortable reaction to the sexual-sounding instruction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to awkward double entendre

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: Open up and put the breast in your mouth.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How you doin'?

jokes.text: Y'know, I don't really know her.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if baby looks scared
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal response avoiding judgment about newborn
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic - completely literal and unhelpful but endearing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Does the baby look scared?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, the baby looks great! I mean, it's all red and wrinkly, but in a good way!

jokes.text: Are you kidding me?!
jokes.setup: Nurse suggests massaging breast to stimulate flow
jokes.punchline: Joey's incredulous reaction to the suggestion in his presence
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective reaction to awkward medical situation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! You're gonna do WHAT in front of me?

jokes.text: I don't know! Maybe she's crazy!
jokes.setup: Rachel frustrated about baby not wanting her breast
jokes.punchline: Joey's unhelpful suggestion that newborn is mentally ill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely unhelpful but delivered with confidence

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The baby won't take my breast. I don't know what's wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Have you tried talking to a psychiatrist? The kid might be crazy.

jokes.text: although, he did eat a piece of plastic fruit earlier.
jokes.setup: Ross defending Joey's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining the defense with specific counterexample
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - sets up defense then immediately destroys it with vivid detail

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey's not stupid! He's actually quite intelligent.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I mean, sure, he thought the capital of France was France, but that's just one thing.

jokes.text: Are you lying? Is this like that time you tried to convince us that you were a doctor?
jokes.setup: Ross denying he proposed
jokes.punchline: Phoebe referencing his history of deception about his profession
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's suspicion of Ross's credibility

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I didn't propose to her!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you're not a paleontologist.

jokes.text: I am a doctor!
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting he's not really a doctor
jokes.punchline: Ross's indignant assertion of his actual profession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the profession confusion with Ross's frustration

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You're not a real doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I have a PhD in paleontology!

jokes.text: Y'know Ross doctors are supposed to be smart.
jokes.setup: Ross confused about the ring situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic dig at Ross's intelligence despite his education
jokes.explanation: Sharp Chandler wit connecting Ross's profession to his current confusion

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A paleontologist and he can't figure out where a ring goes. Yeah, that's not gonna come back to haunt us.

jokes.text: Huh, I might be losing interest in this.
jokes.setup: Confusing explanation of ring situation continues
jokes.punchline: Chandler breaking fourth wall about losing audience interest
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor with Chandler essentially speaking for the audience

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know we've lost the audience.

jokes.text: Oh, what beautiful lukewarm sentiment.
jokes.setup: Ross's halfhearted explanation of his feelings
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic description of Ross's romantic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'lukewarm sentiment' perfectly captures Ross's wishy-washy romantic approach

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, did that come across as not enough enthusiasm? Because I was going for 'guy who just found out his lottery ticket won fifty bucks.'

jokes.text: Did I?
jokes.setup: Ross insisting he didn't propose
jokes.punchline: Sudden doubt about his own actions due to exhaustion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's self-doubt and exhaustion

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I didn't propose to her!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What do you mean you didn't propose? You proposed!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, we all saw you do it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, maybe I did, but I was so tired, I don't remember doing it!

jokes.text: it does sound like something I would do.
jokes.setup: Ross wondering if he accidentally proposed
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his tendency toward impulsive romantic gestures
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment referencing Ross's pattern of romantic impulsiveness

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, did I propose? I don't remember proposing. Did I propose?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, you were on one knee.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That doesn't mean anything! I'm clumsy! I could've tripped!

jokes.text: He didn't make it to one of piano recitals, but this he sees!
jokes.setup: Jack witnessed them having sex
jokes.punchline: Bitter observation about father's selective attendance
jokes.explanation: Relatable family humor with perfect bitter irony about parental priorities

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My father was there for all the important moments in my life. Apparently, this wasn't one of them.

jokes.text: It wasn't fun?!
jokes.setup: Monica explaining sex wasn't for fun
jokes.punchline: Chandler's wounded reaction to implication their sex isn't enjoyable
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Chandler's wounded pride about sexual performance

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. So you're saying our sex isn't fun?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not what I said.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, I heard it. It's not fun. Well, it's fun for me, but you're just lying there like it's a chore, like 'Oh, I have to go to the dentist and have sex with Chandler.'

jokes.text: It was a simpler time.
jokes.setup: Jack lamenting he never thought about them having sex before
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reference to his previous innocence
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery treating lost innocence like historical era

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: I never thought about them having sex before.

jokes.text: Well, get back in there! I'll guard the door!
jokes.setup: Learning they're trying to get pregnant
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic offer to facilitate their conception efforts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jack's over-involvement in his daughter's reproductive efforts

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, if you guys need any help making a baby, I'm there for you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying, I'm a giver.

jokes.text: That's how I got my bad hip.
jokes.setup: Jack describing frequent sex during conception attempts
jokes.punchline: Physical injury as consequence of vigorous reproductive efforts
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with specific physical consequence detail

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: We've been trying to conceive, and let me tell you, the physical toll is real. I've got bruises in places I didn't know could bruise. My chiropractor asked me what happened, and I had to say, 'Well, doc, we're trying to make a baby.' He just nodded like he'd heard it a thousand times.

jokes.text: That's funny, this conversation's how I got the bullet hole in my head.
jokes.setup: Jack sharing TMI about conception efforts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dark joke about wanting to shoot himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Chandler dark humor - mirrors Jack's structure while expressing horror

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more traumatized?

jokes.text: You up to the task sailor?
jokes.setup: Jack discussing importance of female orgasm for conception
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate challenge to Chandler about sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment with inappropriate father-in-law behavior

jokes.text: Seriously sir, my brains? All over the wall.
jokes.setup: Jack's inappropriate sexual challenge
jokes.punchline: Chandler's graphic description of wanting to commit suicide
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the suicide joke with vivid imagery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I want to kill myself.

jokes.text: Well, now's a good time. I'm on my way to have my ears cut off.
jokes.setup: Joey wants to tell Monica something
jokes.punchline: Monica's hyperbolic description of escaping the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Monica's desperation to escape TMI

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, I need to tell you something.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, if you're about to tell me you slept with my sister again, I'm going to need to sit down. Actually, I'm already sitting. I'm going to need to lie down. Actually, I'm going to need to leave the country, fake my own death, and start a new life in a remote village where nobody knows my name or my humiliating family.

jokes.text: It's-it's so inappropriate. No, it's worse than that. It's wrong. It's… It is bigger than mine!
jokes.setup: Monica listing why Joey/Rachel engagement is wrong
jokes.punchline: Escalating moral objections suddenly derailed by ring envy
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds moral outrage then undercuts with petty jealousy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is wrong! You barely know each other! You're not ready for marriage! You're—wait, is that a diamond?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Let me see that ring.

jokes.text: Days of Our Lives, thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Monica admiring Joey's ring
jokes.punchline: Monica attributing quality to Joey's soap opera success
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to Joey's career with good character knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Joey, this ring is beautiful!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Thanks, I got it on the show.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, it's gorgeous. You must be doing really well on 'Days of Our Lives.'

jokes.text: Sure. / Sure? / Yeah, I mean whatever.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Rachel loves Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly lukewarm responses revealing her true feelings
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure showing Rachel's declining enthusiasm through repetition

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you love Joey?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Of course I love Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I mean do you love him love him?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I... well, I mean, he's great.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? He's... he's fine.

jokes.text: No! She had the ring on, she seemed so excited, and then she took her breast out.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why Joey didn't tell Rachel the truth
jokes.punchline: Joey's escalating list of distractions ending with breast exposure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with callback to earlier awkward moment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I was going to tell her, but then I saw this thing on TV, and then Monica needed help moving a couch, and then I got hungry, and then... I saw a really nice pair of... you know what, I just got distracted, okay?

jokes.text: Joey, you have to tell her what's going on! And what did it look like?!
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Joey serious advice about the situation
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to prurient curiosity about Rachel's breast
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Chandler's priorities shifting mid-advice

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you need to think about this carefully. This is serious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, uh... how was it?

jokes.text: I didn't look at it. Stupid baby's head was blocking most of it.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what Rachel's breast looked like
jokes.punchline: Joey's frustrated complaint about baby obstructing his view
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - calling baby 'stupid' for blocking his view is perfectly inappropriate and hilarious

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! The baby's in the way!

jokes.text: Oh, by the way. Two people screwing in there if you want to check that out.
jokes.setup: Joey finished with serious conversation about being gentle
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning sex happening as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment with characteristic casual delivery of shocking information

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, you gotta be gentle, you know? Take your time, really listen to what she wants...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, and then you have sex.

jokes.text: Poor baby, you're so tired.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to explain the proposal confusion
jokes.punchline: Rachel patronizingly treating Ross like a confused child
jokes.explanation: Good condescending delivery showing Rachel's misplaced confidence

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wasn't confused! I knew exactly what I was doing!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, sweetie. That's nice. Do you want some juice?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, I'm not five years old!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Of course you're not, honey. You're a big boy. A very smart big boy.

jokes.text: I can come back.
jokes.setup: Walking into heated argument about who proposed
jokes.punchline: Joey's understated response to obvious chaos
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understatement of obvious situation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm just gonna grab some coffee and pretend I didn't hear any of this.

jokes.text: Whoa! Hey! Whoa-whoa-whoa, you saw her breast?! / I'll tell you about it later. Be cool.
jokes.setup: Ross getting upset about the proposal situation
jokes.punchline: Suddenly shifting priorities to voyeuristic interest, Joey's casual conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing male priorities and Joey treating it like juicy gossip

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't believe this is happening!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, is Chandler gonna be naked?

jokes.text: Dude, step up! I proposed.
jokes.setup: Ross being wishy-washy about his romantic intentions
jokes.punchline: Joey claiming credit for decisive romantic action he didn't actually take
jokes.explanation: Great Joey moment - taking credit for romantic boldness while knowing it was accidental

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! You see what I did? I went over there, I told her how I felt, I made a move. That's what you gotta do, Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you didn't do any of that.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, but I'm telling you about it like I did, and that's just as good.

jokes.text: There's a lot going on here and I think I ate some bad fruit earlier.
jokes.setup: Joey just claimed he proposed
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing down and blaming confusion on digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic connecting his confusion to earlier digestive mistake

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. I didn't propose. I was just... I had some bad shrimp earlier and I think the indigestion was making me say crazy things.

jokes.text: Usually after I have a baby with a woman I like to slow things down!
jokes.setup: Ross saying he doesn't want to rush into anything
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic observation about the backwards timing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler sarcasm pointing out the illogical relationship timeline

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is, you don't want to rush into anything, but you've already slept with her?

jokes.text: Man! That kid is going to town!
jokes.setup: Joey apologizing and being sincere
jokes.punchline: Suddenly distracted by enthusiastic breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his distractibility and inappropriate commentary

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm really sorry. I mean it. I was wrong and I—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Is that... are you...? Oh my God, that's amazing!

jokes.text: Sorry to bother you again, but could you pass my pamphlets back?
jokes.setup: Jack previously brought inappropriate pregnancy pamphlets
jokes.punchline: Awkward request to retrieve sex education materials from romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains Jack's inappropriate involvement

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Hey, remember those pregnancy pamphlets I brought? Well, I need you to go get them from my bedroom.

dialogue.speaker: Other character
dialogue.line: Now? We're in the middle of something!

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: I know, I know, but I really need them right now.

tmdb_overview: Following Rachel's acceptance of Joey's accidental proposal, confusion reigns as Rachel wonders whether it was the right thing to do and Phoebe can't seem to mind her own business. Finally, Rachel, Ross and Joey get together and figure out that no one was actually going to propose. Meanwhile, Mr. Geller walks in on Monica and Chandler having sex in a closet.
insight: Season premiere delivers steady laughs with character-driven cringe, 58 jokes in 19 minutes.
